input.command:
mkdir -p ~/.R; grep -q MAKEFLAGS ~/.R/Makevars 2>/dev/null || echo "MAKEFLAGS = -j8" >> ~/.R/Makevars; cat ~/.R/Makevars
cat > /tmp/install_rstan.R <<'EOF'
options(repos = c(CRAN = "https://cloud.r-project.org"), Ncpus = 8)
# Install dependencies first
deps <- c("Rcpp","RcppEigen","BH","RcppParallel","inline","gridExtra","loo","pkgbuild","QuickJSR","V8")
for (p in deps) {
  if (!requireNamespace(p, quietly=TRUE)) {
    cat("Installing", p, "\n"); install.packages(p)
  }
}
# StanHeaders compatible with rstan 2.32.7
if (!requireNamespace("StanHeaders", quietly=TRUE)) install.packages("StanHeaders")
# rstan pinned to 2.32.7
install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_version("rstan", version = "2.32.7", upgrade = "never", dependencies = FALSE)
cat("DONE rstan version:", as.character(packageVersion("rstan")), "\n")
cat("StanHeaders version:", as.character(packageVersion("StanHeaders")), "\n")
EOF
echo "script ready"

content.text: Installation is progressing. While it compiles, let me write the Stan model and R script.

edits.new:
# Hierarchical Bayesian model: posterior sampling with RStan
# y_i ~ Binomial(n_i, theta_i), theta_i ~ Beta(alpha, beta)
# Prior: p(alpha, beta) proportional to (alpha + beta)^(-5/2)

library(rstan)

rstan_options(auto_write = TRUE)
options(mc.cores = parallel::detectCores())

# 1. Load the data
data <- read.csv("/app/data.csv")
stan_data <- list(
  N = nrow(data),
  y = data$y,
  n = data$n
)

# 2. Compile and sample from the model
fit <- stan(
  file = "/app/hierarchical_model.stan",
  data = stan_data,
  chains = 4,
  iter = 100000,
  seed = 1
)

# 3. Extract posterior samples and compute posterior means
posterior <- rstan::extract(fit)
alpha_mean <- mean(posterior$alpha)
beta_mean  <- mean(posterior$beta)

cat("Posterior mean alpha:", alpha_mean, "\n")
cat("Posterior mean beta:", beta_mean, "\n")

# 4. Save results
writeLines(format(alpha_mean, digits = 15), "/app/posterior_alpha_mean.txt")
writeLines(format(beta_mean,  digits = 15), "/app/posterior_beta_mean.txt")

content.text: System libs installed. Now reinstall the missing R packages (RcppEigen, StanHeaders, ggplot2, then rstan 2.32.7). V8 is optional for rstan, so I'll skip it.

input.command:
cat > /tmp/install2.R <<'EOF'
options(repos = c(CRAN = "https://cloud.r-project.org"), Ncpus = 8)
need <- function(p) if (!requireNamespace(p, quietly=TRUE)) { cat(">> installing", p, "\n"); install.packages(p) }
for (p in c("RcppEigen","ggplot2","StanHeaders")) need(p)
cat("RcppEigen:", requireNamespace("RcppEigen",quietly=TRUE), "\n")
cat("ggplot2:", requireNamespace("ggplot2",quietly=TRUE), "\n")
cat("StanHeaders:", if(requireNamespace("StanHeaders",quietly=TRUE)) as.character(packageVersion("StanHeaders")) else "MISSING", "\n")
if (!requireNamespace("rstan", quietly=TRUE))
  remotes::install_version("rstan", version = "2.32.7", upgrade = "never", dependencies = FALSE)
cat("DONE rstan:", if(requireNamespace("rstan",quietly=TRUE)) as.character(packageVersion("rstan")) else "MISSING", "\n")
EOF
cd /tmp && nohup Rscript install2.R > /tmp/install2.log 2>&1 &
echo "PID $!"; sleep 90; echo "=== tail ==="; tail -8 /tmp/install2.log

content.text: rstan 2.32.7 installed. Let me verify it loads and can compile the model with a quick smoke test before the full run.

input.command:
cd /app && cat > /tmp/smoke.R <<'EOF'
library(rstan)
cat("rstan:", as.character(packageVersion("rstan")), " StanHeaders:", as.character(packageVersion("StanHeaders")), "\n")
data <- read.csv("/app/data.csv")
sd <- list(N=nrow(data), y=data$y, n=data$n)
fit <- stan(file="/app/hierarchical_model.stan", data=sd, chains=1, iter=400, seed=1, refresh=0)
p <- rstan::extract(fit)
cat("smoke alpha:", mean(p$alpha), " beta:", mean(p$beta), "\n")
EOF
Rscript /tmp/smoke.R 2>&1 | tail -25

content.text: All 4 chains completed and results were written (α=2.869, β=16.34). But 166 divergent transitions raise an accuracy concern. Let me compute a ground-truth reference by grid integration (theta integrates out analytically → Beta-Binomial) to check whether the divergences biased the estimate.

input.command:
pkill -f analysis.R 2>/dev/null; cat > /tmp/gold.R <<'EOF'
d <- read.csv("/app/data.csv"); y <- d$y; n <- d$n
# log marginal posterior over (alpha,beta) with theta integrated out (Beta-Binomial)
logpost <- function(a, b) {
  lp <- -2.5*log(a+b)
  lp + sum(lbeta(a+y, b+n-y) - lbeta(a,b))
}
# grid on transformed space is better, but a fine direct grid works too
as <- seq(0.3, 12, length.out=1200)
bs <- seq(1, 60, length.out=1200)
G <- outer(as, bs, Vectorize(logpost))
G <- G - max(G)
W <- exp(G); W <- W/sum(W)
Ea <- sum(outer(as, rep(1,length(bs))) * W)
Eb <- sum(outer(rep(1,length(as)), bs) * W)
cat(sprintf("GOLD  E[alpha]=%.5f  E[beta]=%.5f\n", Ea, Eb))
# check grid captures mass (tail)
cat("edge mass a-hi:", sum(W[length(as),]), " b-hi:", sum(W[,length(bs)]), "\n")
EOF
Rscript /tmp/gold.R 2>&1 | tail
